# omada_wan (Data Source)

Reads the gateway's WAN port configuration.

This is intentionally **read-only**. The controller's WAN endpoint is a single large document mixing configuration with read-only capability flags, and a bad write drops the site's internet connection — which makes the write path unsafe to validate against a live gateway. Use this data source to assert on or reference the current WAN settings; change them in the Omada UI.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# WAN settings are read-only — see the data source docs for why.
data "omada_wan" "gateway" {}

output "wan_proto" {
  value = data.omada_wan.gateway.ports[0].proto
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `site` (String) Site name to query (resolved to an ID). Ignored if `site_id` is set.
- `site_id` (String) Site ID to query. Provide this or `site`; if neither is set, the provider's default site is used.

### Read-Only

- `ports` (Attributes List) The gateway's WAN ports. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--ports))

<a id="nestedatt--ports"></a>
### Nested Schema for `ports`

Read-Only:

- `ipv6_enable` (Number) Whether IPv6 is enabled on the port (0 = off).
- `mac_address` (String) MAC address in use on the WAN port.
- `mac_method` (String) MAC address mode: `recover` (factory), `clone`, or `custom`.
- `mtu` (Number) MTU of the IPv4 connection.
- `port_name` (String) Port name, e.g. `WAN/LAN1`.
- `port_uuid` (String) Controller UUID of the physical port.
- `proto` (String) IPv4 connection type: `dhcp`, `pppoe`, `static`, ...
- `qos_tag_enable` (Boolean) Whether an 802.1p QoS tag is set on the WAN link.
- `vlan_id` (Number) VLAN tag applied to the WAN link (0 = untagged).
